dataset.title: TWIC Midlothian Council – LBS Site Surveys

abstract.para: Species data generated from survey work undertaken on Local Biodiversity Sites by TWIC on behalf of Midlothian Council (contracted surveys). These are predominantly botanical site surveys, with incidental species records for other taxa collected as part of the site visits. Surveys are undertaken by TWIC staff, sometimes with a volunteer assisting.

additionalInfo.para: Recorder names withheld. TBC Badger records have been withheld due to sensitive species concerns – as these are site-based surveys, the locations are readily identifiable even after blurring the grid references. The records are available from TWIC as part of their data search service.

intellectualRights.para: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution (CC-BY) 4.0 License.

purpose.para: To support the assessment of Local Biodiversity Sites (LBS) in Midlothian Council area following national guidance on Local Nature Conservation Sites.

description.para:
Site surveys are undertaken during the survey season - May to August (September). A comprehensive vascular plant species list is generated for the site. The surveyor compiles a list of vascular plants for each 1km square within the Local Biodiversity Site boundary.
For notable, protected and Invasive Non-native Species (INNS), a minimum of 100m square reference is usually provided for the sighting.
Records for other taxa observed on site, especially invasive non-native species (INNS) and protected species, if present, are also collected on an ad hoc basis.

Surveys are undertaken by an experienced ecologist and botanist. Plant specimens are taken for confirmation where necessary. Data are checked using NBN Record Cleaner and are shared with the Botanical Society of Britain and Ireland (BSBI) recorders who can flag verification issues. The quality of the data is expected to be high. Non-vascular plant data is checked using TWIC’s normal verification procedures - records pending verification may be less reliable.

gbif.citation: The Wildlife Information Centre (2026). TWIC Midlothian Council – LBS Site Surveys. Occurrence dataset https://doi.org/10.15468/bqwpq6 accessed via GBIF.org on 2026-05-22.
